description_long: This is a rectangular, dual-compartment street cabinet made of metal, commonly found on urban sidewalks for housing electrical or telecommunications equipment. The front features two vertical doors, each with a handle and a visible locking mechanism, as well as warning labels indicating electrical hazard. The cabinet is predominantly off-white or light gray, with visible dirt, weathering, and graffiti on both the front and back surfaces. The sides are plain, with vertical grooves for structural reinforcement, and no visible openings or features. The back is heavily marked with black and purple graffiti, and the top surface is flat and slightly weathered, suitable for supporting small objects.
description: A weathered, dual-door metal street cabinet with graffiti, warning labels, and locking handles, typically used for electrical or telecom infrastructure.
description_view_0: Front view showing two doors with handles, warning labels, graffiti, and informational stickers.
description_view_1: Left side view, plain with vertical grooves and no visible features or markings.
description_view_2: Back view, heavily covered in black and purple graffiti, no handles or labels.
description_view_3: Right side view, plain with vertical grooves, similar to the left side.
